summary: A correctly positioned but thin process guide that restates a subset of the quality-evaluation blueprint's enforcement rules without adding the examples, edge cases, or operational nuance that process-blueprints.agentish.ts defines as the companion guide's purpose. The file is actively paired with a live JSON contract and in use, so obsolescence risk is low, but the file currently contributes minimal incremental value over its parent blueprint.
strengths: Clear self-description: immediately identifies its role as companion guide to the paired JSON process blueprint
Rules are unambiguous and actionable — low interpretive slack on each individual enforcement point
Correctly layered: references the language root, does not overreach into JSON contract territory, stays at guide-level abstraction
Actively paired with a live JSON contract that has watchdog fields, completion tokens, and stop conditions — concrete live-use evidence
weaknesses: Enforcement rules largely duplicate content already present in agentish-quality-evaluation.agentish.ts — the file adds almost no semantic content the corpus did not already own
No when/then causal patterns despite Agentish supporting them — the guide is a flat imperative list rather than a topology-preserving artifact
process-blueprints.agentish.ts defines companion guides as covering 'process semantics, examples, and edge cases for agents and operators' — this file provides none of those
No definitions block, no worked examples of tricky file-class judgments, no guidance on how to handle ambiguous classification — the situations where a guide would add the most value
